prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for acrolein (CH2=CH–CHO) were computed with the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill using DFT at tier "standard" — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) as implemented in PySCF 2.5 — in the gas phase.  The calculation was a single-point evaluation on the user-supplied acrolein.xyz geometry (no additional optimization; DFT//input-XYZ).  Charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral state; the cation (N–1) and anion (N+1) states were both treated as doublets (charge +1/–1, multiplicity 2) as per the skill default.  All three SCF calculations converged and the engine reported integrity status "ok" with trustworthy = true.

Most electrophilic site: atom 4 (C, index 3) with f⁺ = 0.64232102.
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.39388616.

Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).  No geometry relaxation was performed.

Live run log was streamed to the default `<subcommand>_<timestamp>.out`; see the path echoed during execution for full SCF details and optimizer progress.  The plotted bar chart of atomic Fukui functions is available at:
/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/argo_o3/20260704-174603_acrolein_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png

Warnings (verbatim from engine):

• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

These indicate the expected normalization checks passed (sums exactly 1.0000) and do not signal convergence problems.

Method–provenance summary:
Property: Atomic Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor (most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard), no RI density fitting (exact integrals)
Geometry: user-provided acrolein.xyz (not re-optimized)
Solvent/environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge/multiplicity: 0 / 1 (neutral), +1 / 2 (cation), –1 / 2 (anion)
Software: PySCF 2.5 via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill
Convergence: all SCFs converged; integrity status ok, trustworthy true.

Report generated from computed data; no experimental comparison requested.
